driver.description: Database driver for this service. By default postgres is used.

version.description: Database version for this service, that will be used to setup database. By default latest version is used.

host.description: Database host for this service, that will be used in DB_HOST. By default localhost is used.

user.description: Database user for this service, that will be used in DB_USER.

password.description: Database password for this service, that will be used in DB_PASSWORD.

databaseName.description: Database name for this service, that will be used in DB_NAME.

port.description: Database port for this service, that will be used in DB_PORT.

port2.description: Secondary port for this database service.

manualRun.description: Determines if the service will be run with run cmd. If it is true, then to run you add --services manual_to_run_service to run cmd. By default it is false.

seedFromDbEnvPath.description: Path to .env file with db credentials for db, from which data is seeded. Use either seedFromFilePath or seedFromDb/seedFromDbEnvPath.

seedFromFilePath.description: Path to dump.sql file from which data is seeded. Use either seedFromFilePath or seedFromDb/seedFromDbEnvPath.

seedFromDb.description: Db credentials to seed from. Use either seedFromFilePath or seedFromDb/seedFromDbEnvPath.

host.description: Host of the database to seed from.

databaseName.description: Name of the database to seed from.

user.description: User for the database to seed from.

password.description: Password for the database to seed from.

port.description: Port of the database to seed from.

definitionPath.description: Path to definition file (e.g., RabbitMQ definitions JSON file)

services.description: localstack driver only. AWS services to enable in the single LocalStack container (e.g. [sqs, s3]).

queues.description: localstack driver only. SQS queues to auto-create in the LocalStack bootstrap.

buckets.description: localstack: S3 buckets to auto-create in the LocalStack bootstrap. supabase: Storage buckets to create via Storage API + emit per-bucket env (SUPABASE_BUCKET_<NAME>=<bucket>).

jwtSecret.description: supabase driver only. Override the stock JWT secret. Must match auth.jwt_secret in supabase/config.toml — corgi re-signs ANON_KEY / SERVICE_ROLE_KEY with this so emitted env matches what `supabase status` reports.

configTomlPath.description: supabase driver only. Path (relative to corgi-compose.yml) to the canonical config.toml. When set, corgi copies it to corgi_services/db_services/<svc>/supabase/config.toml on every init and runs the supabase CLI from there. When unset, falls back to <projectRoot>/supabase/config.toml created by `supabase init`.

dbPort.description: supabase driver only. Override [db].port in config.toml. Patched on every up so emitted SUPABASE_DB_PORT matches the bind. Default 54322.

inbucketPort.description: supabase driver only. Override [inbucket].port (local email capture UI). Default 54324.

image.description: image driver only. Docker image reference, e.g. `gotenberg/gotenberg:8`.

containerPort.description: image driver only. Container's internal port. Defaults to `port:` if unset. Used in docker-compose `<port>:<containerPort>` mapping.

environment.description: image driver only. Docker-compose environment list, e.g. ["MEILI_MASTER_KEY=secret", "LOG_LEVEL=debug"]. Passed through verbatim.

volumes.description: image driver only. Docker-compose volume mounts, e.g. ["./data:/app/data", "named-vol:/var/lib/x"]. Passed through verbatim.

command.description: image driver only. Override container entrypoint args, e.g. ["--collector.zipkin.host-port=9411"]. Passed through verbatim as docker-compose `command:` array.

authUsers.description: supabase driver only. Auth users to seed via the supabase Admin API on `up`. Idempotent — duplicates are skipped.

metadata.description: Map serialized to JSON for user_metadata. e.g. { role: admin, plan: pro }.

topics.description: localstack driver only. SNS topics to auto-create in the LocalStack bootstrap. Implies the 'sns' service.

subscriptions.description: localstack driver only. SNS topic -> SQS queue subscriptions. Each entry must reference a topic from 'topics' and a queue from 'queues'.

topic.description: SNS topic name. Must be declared in 'topics'.

queue.description: SQS queue name. Must be declared in 'queues'.

secrets.description: localstack driver only. AWS Secrets Manager entries to auto-create. Implies the 'secretsmanager' service. Local dev only — do not commit real production secrets.

parameters.description: localstack driver only. AWS SSM Parameter Store entries to auto-create. Implies the 'ssm' service.

streams.description: localstack driver only. Kinesis streams to auto-create (1 shard each). Implies the 'kinesis' service.

healthCheck.description: Optional HTTP path probed by `corgi status`. If set, status GETs http://localhost:<port><healthCheck> and accepts any non-5xx response as healthy. If unset, status falls back to a raw TCP connect on the port. localstack driver defaults to /_localstack/health.

profiles.description: Named profiles this db_service belongs to. corgi run --profile <name> runs only members of that profile plus their transitive depends_on closure.

path.description: Path to the actual project repo. By default the path to the folder in which corgi-compose.yml is used.

ignore_env.description: Should service ignore env and don't change env file or not. By default is false (env is not ignored).

manualRun.description: Determines if the service will be run with run cmd. If it is true, that to run you add --services manual_to_run_service to run cmd. By default it is false.

cloneFrom.description: Git url to target repo. By default nothing is cloned.

branch.description: Branch to use for git checkout. By default default branch for repo is used.

environment.description:
List of environment variables to add to this service's .env file.

Each entry is `KEY=VALUE`. The VALUE supports two substitution forms:
  • `${OWN_VAR}` — references another env var on the same service (own copyEnv, db emissions, port).
  • `${producer.VAR}` — references a var exported by another service. The producer must be listed in this service's depends_on_services AND export VAR via its `exports:` list.

Example:
  - DATABASE_URL=postgresql://${DB_USER}:${DB_PASSWORD}@${DB_HOST}:${DB_PORT}/${DB_NAME}
  - NOTIFICATION_SERVICE_TOKEN=${notification-api.NOTIFICATION_SERVICE_TOKEN}

envPath.description: Path to .env file in target repo. By default .env file is used.

copyEnvFromFilePath.description: The path to the .env, which content will be copied to service repo .env file.

localhostNameInEnv.description: Localhost name to use in .env file, by default localhost is used.

port.description: Service port, that will be added to .env file.

portAlias.description: Service port env name alias, that will be added to .env file. By default PORT is used, e.g. PORT=5432.

tunnel.description: Optional named (stable URL) tunnel for `corgi tunnel`. When set + hostname resolves non-empty, corgi runs the provider in named mode. Otherwise falls back to Quick mode.

provider.description: Tunnel provider. cloudflared (Named Tunnel, free with Cloudflare-managed domain) or ngrok (free static domain on *.ngrok-free.app). localtunnel rejected — Quick mode only. CLI --provider flag overrides this.

hostname.description: Public HTTPS hostname (e.g. api-andrii.dev.example.com or my-static.ngrok-free.app). Supports ${VAR} substitution from shell env, then service env file. Required when block present.

name.description: cloudflared only: tunnel name (must already exist via `cloudflared tunnel create <name>`). Supports ${VAR} substitution. Ignored for ngrok.

depends_on_services.description: Adds service credentials to .env. suffix is added at the end of added value. NAME_TO_USE_IN_ENV=localhost:port/special/suffix will be added to .env. If you add just name, than it is SERVICE_NAME=localhost:port_in_env.

name.description: Name of the service to depend on.

envAlias.description: Name to use in environment variable.

suffix.description: Suffix to add at the end of the service URL.

condition.description: ready (default): wait until the dependency passes its readiness probe before starting this service. started: wait only until it has launched.

exports.description: Whitelist of env vars this service exports to dependents. Entry 'NAME' re-exports an env var from the service's own resolved env. Entry 'NAME=value' is an inline literal (supports ${OWN_VAR} expansion). Dependents reference these via ${producer.NAME} inside their own `environment` block. Producer must be listed in consumer's depends_on_services.

depends_on_db.description: Adds db credentials (DB_HOST,etc) from db_services will be copied to .env. envAlias adds string before db credentials, like NAME_BEFORE_DB_IN_ENV_DB_HOST.

name.description: Name of the database service to depend on.

envAlias.description: String to add before db credentials in environment variables.

condition.description: ready (default): wait until the dependency passes its readiness probe before starting this service. started: wait only until it has launched.

beforeStart.description: List of commands to run consequently, before start commands are run.

start.description: List of commands to run in parallel for the service needs.

afterStart.description: List of commands to run consequently, when the cli is exited.

interactiveInput.description: Enable interactive input for the service.

autoSourceEnv.description: Toggle automatic sourcing of the service .env before start/beforeStart commands. Default true. Set false to keep secrets out of subprocess env (e.g. npm postinstall).

healthCheck.description: Optional HTTP path probed by `corgi status`. If set, status GETs http://localhost:<port><healthCheck> and accepts any non-5xx response as healthy. If unset, status falls back to a raw TCP connect on the port.

scripts.description: List of scripts to run for the service.

manualRun.description: Whether the script should be run manually.

commands.description: List of commands to execute in this script.

copyEnvFromFilePath.description: Path to .env file to copy environment variables from for this script.

profiles.description: Named profiles this service belongs to. corgi run --profile <name> runs only members of that profile plus their transitive depends_on closure.

required.description: Required commands and their installation instructions.

why.description: The reasons to use/install this required command.

install.description: Installation steps to run, if cmd not found.

optional.description: Show or not the prompt, before this cmd installation. By default false.

checkCmd.description: Command to run to check, if it is installed. By default cmd name is used.

init.description: Initialization commands to run at the start.

beforeStart.description: Commands to run before starting services.

afterStart.description: Commands to run after starting services.

useDocker.description: Whether to use Docker for running services.

useAwsVpn.description: Whether to use AWS VPN connection.
